title: React API Calls – Fetching & Managing Remote Data

meta.title: React API Integration Guide – Fetch, Axios & Async Patterns (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to fetch data from APIs in React. Master the Fetch API, Axios, handling loading/error states, and using useEffect for side effects.

content.value: In modern web apps, components often need to display data from a server. In React, API calls are considered 'side effects' because they interact with the outside world. These are typically handled inside the `useEffect` hook to ensure they run at the correct point in the component lifecycle.
You have two primary choices for making HTTP requests: the native browser `fetch()` API or the popular third-party library `Axios`.
A professional API implementation must handle three distinct states: Loading (the request is in flight), Error (the request failed), and Data (the request succeeded).

value: **Cleanup after yourself**: Use an `AbortController` to cancel requests if the component unmounts before the request finishes.
**Abstract your API logic**: Move fetch calls to a separate `services/` folder to keep components clean.
**Avoid 'Waterfall' fetches**: If two API calls don't depend on each other, use `Promise.all()` to fetch them in parallel.
**Consider a library**: For complex apps, use **TanStack Query** (React Query) to handle caching, re-fetching, and background updates automatically.

faq.question: Why do I see my API call running twice?
faq.answer: This usually happens in React Strict Mode during development. React intentionally mounts, unmounts, and re-mounts components to help you find side-effect bugs.

faq.question: How do I send a POST request with Fetch?
faq.answer: Pass an options object as the second argument to fetch with `method: 'POST'`, `headers`, and `body: JSON.stringify(data)`.

faq.question: What is an Interceptor in Axios?
faq.answer: It's a function that runs before a request is sent (e.g., to add an Auth token) or after a response is received (e.g., to handle 401 errors globally).

quiz.question: Which hook is most appropriate for initiating an API call on component load?
What happens if you forget to add an empty dependency array [] to a fetch useEffect?
options: The API call runs only once
The API call runs on every single render (Infinite loop potential)
The browser will block the request

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of using Axios over the native Fetch API?
options: It automatically transforms JSON data and handles errors more gracefully
It is made by the React team
